behavior_in_task_description.reason: All tested artifacts (build-all script, build-native-auto, turbo.jsonc, AGENTS.md mentions of pnpm build vs build-all) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to rename/add but doesn't give exact line-level diffs; agent must still construct the correct JSON/config structure.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for script names and file existence, not exact text; alternative wording for AGENTS.md still passes as long as it mentions the required phrases.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node and prettier subprocesses, parse JSON, and inspect script contents — not pure text grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific script names (build-all, build-native-auto), specific file rename (turbo.jsonc), and content checks; stubs would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover root build-all script, next-swc script rename, turbo.jsonc existence, and AGENTS.md content.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named descriptively with docstrings and grouped with header comments (pass_to_pass section).

structured_data_schema.reason: No structured data output beyond standard package.json scripts, which are described.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and script names are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files but these are intrinsic to the task domain; doesn't give line-level fixes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: AGENTS.md diff is localized to a few lines around build commands, not a rewrite.
